Examples.Title: Create a channel connection
Examples.Description: Create a new channel connection for a resource for given integration. Only one channel connection is allowed per resource and integration.
Examples.Code:
### Create a channel connection
# @name ChannelConnectionsController_createChannelConnection
POST {{host}}/v1/channel-connections
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "identifier": "slack-prod-user123-abc4",
  "subscriberId": "subscriber-123",
  "context": {},
  "connectionMode": "shared",
  "integrationIdentifier": "slack-prod",
  "workspace": {
    "id": "T123456",
    "name": "Acme HQ"
  },
  "auth": {
    "accessToken": "Workspace access token"
  }
}

Examples.Title: Create a context
Examples.Description:
Create a new context with the specified type, id, and data. Returns 409 if context already exists.
      **type** and **id** are required fields, **data** is optional, if the context already exists, it returns the 409 response
Examples.Code:
### Create a context
# @name ContextsController_createContext
POST {{host}}/v2/contexts
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "type": "tenant",
  "id": "org-acme",
  "data": {
    "tenantName": "Acme Corp",
    "region": "us-east-1",
    "settings": {
      "theme": "dark"

}

      **type** and **id** are optional fields, if provided, only contexts with the matching type and id will be returned.

Examples.Title: Create a variable
Examples.Description: Creates a new environment variable. Keys must be uppercase with underscores only (e.g. BASE_URL). Secret variables are encrypted at rest and masked in API responses.
Examples.Code:
### Create a variable
# @name EnvironmentVariablesController_createEnvironmentVariable
POST {{host}}/v1/environment-variables
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "key": "string",
  "type": "string",
  "isSecret": true,
  "values": [

      "_environmentId": "string",
      "value": "string"

Examples.Title: Trigger event
Examples.Description:
Trigger event is the main (and only) way to send notifications to subscribers. The trigger identifier is used to match the particular workflow associated with it. Maximum number of recipients can be 100. Additional information can be passed according the body interface below.
    To prevent duplicate triggers, you can optionally pass a **transactionId** in the request body. If the same **transactionId** is used again, the trigger will be ignored. The retention period depends on your billing tier.
Examples.Code:
### Trigger event
# @name EventsController_trigger
POST {{host}}/v1/events/trigger
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "name": "workflow_identifier",
  "payload": {
    "comment_id": "string",
    "post": {
      "text": "string"
    }
  },
  "overrides": {
    "fcm": {
      "data": {
        "key": "value"
      }
    }
  },
  "to": [
    {
      "firstName": "John",
      "lastName": "Doe",
      "email": "john.doe@example.com",
      "phone": "\u002B1234567890",
      "avatar": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg",
      "locale": "en-US",
      "timezone": "America/New_York",
      "data": {},
      "subscriberId": "string",
      "channels": []
    }
  ],
  "transactionId": "string",
  "actor": "string",
  "tenant": "string",
  "context": {}
}
